question.prompt: When did the first DLP alerts for the anomalous account appear relative to the baseline period?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: DLP alerts for jmorris first elevated above the organizational baseline during the early signal phase, approximately day 10-11, when the alert rate rose to 2-3 per day versus the org average of 0.5 per user per day.
key_facts: data transfer volumes from a single account diverge from peer group norms in a sustained multi-week pattern
lateral information gathering pattern inconsistent with declared operational role

answer.answer_text:
The baseline period for DLP monitoring is established from the initial days of September 2024, with normal user activity observed starting on 2024-09-02 [insider_threat_05_ep_001]. On that day, DLP alerts were generated for multiple users, including lpatel, who triggered a MEDIUM severity alert under the BULK_DOWNLOAD_POLICY rule [insider_threat_05_ep_001].

The first anomalous DLP alerts associated with suspicious behavior appear on 2024-09-15, when user jmorris triggered multiple alerts, including for SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH, ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT, and CLOUD_UPLOAD_POLICY, all involving transfers to box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_014]. These alerts were notable and required review, especially as they occurred during a red team exercise, which complicated attribution [insider_threat_05_ep_014].

Therefore, the first DLP alerts for the anomalous account (jmorris) appeared on 2024-09-15, which is 13 days after the baseline period established on 2024-09-02 [insider_threat_05_ep_001][insider_threat_05_ep_014].

turns.content:
The baseline period for DLP monitoring is established from the initial days of September 2024, with normal user activity observed starting on 2024-09-02 [insider_threat_05_ep_001]. On that day, DLP alerts were generated for multiple users, including lpatel, who triggered a MEDIUM severity alert under the BULK_DOWNLOAD_POLICY rule [insider_threat_05_ep_001].

The first anomalous DLP alerts associated with suspicious behavior appear on 2024-09-15, when user jmorris triggered multiple alerts, including for SENSITIVE_KEYWORD_MATCH, ENCRYPTED_ARCHIVE_DETECT, and CLOUD_UPLOAD_POLICY, all involving transfers to box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_014]. Yes, there is sufficient evidence to escalate the anomalous access patterns to the security incident response team.

User jmorris exhibits a consistent pattern of high-risk behavior across multiple days. On 2024-09-10, jmorris accessed 12 repositories, including sensitive projects like project-atlas and project-sentinel, and downloaded 78 files totaling 187.7 MB [insider_threat_05_ep_009]. This activity triggered two DLP alerts for sensitive keyword matches involving email attachments, indicating potential data exfiltration [insider_threat_05_ep_009]. On 2024-09-11, jmorris again accessed 9 repositories and downloaded 49 files totaling 231.7 MB, with two additional DLP alerts for file size threshold violations when uploading to SharePoint [insider_threat_05_ep_010]. The activity continued on 2024-09-13, with 46 files downloaded (374.5 MB) and two more DLP alerts for large file uploads to Box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_012]. Most critically, on 2024-09-16, jmorris downloaded 61 files totaling 512.9 MB and triggered three DLP alerts, including one for a sensitive keyword match and another for a cloud upload policy violation, both involving Box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_015].

This escalating pattern of excessive data access, repeated large downloads, and multiple DLP violations across different cloud destinations constitutes a clear and growing risk. The behavior is anomalous compared to peers and aligns with data exfiltration tactics, warranting immediate escalation to the security incident response team for further investigation [insider_threat_05_ep_009][insider_threat_05_ep_010][insider_threat_05_ep_012][insider_threat_05_ep_015].

Yes, there is sufficient evidence to escalate the anomalous access patterns to the security incident response team.

User jmorris exhibits a consistent pattern of high-risk behavior across multiple days. On 2024-09-10, jmorris accessed 12 repositories, including sensitive projects like project-atlas and project-sentinel, and downloaded 78 files totaling 187.7 MB [insider_threat_05_ep_009]. This activity triggered two DLP alerts for sensitive keyword matches involving email attachments, indicating potential data exfiltration [insider_threat_05_ep_009]. On 2024-09-11, jmorris again accessed 9 repositories and downloaded 49 files totaling 231.7 MB, with two additional DLP alerts for file size threshold violations when uploading to SharePoint [insider_threat_05_ep_010]. The activity continued on 2024-09-13, with 46 files downloaded (374.5 MB) and two more DLP alerts for large file uploads to Box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_012]. Most critically, on 2024-09-16, jmorris downloaded 61 files totaling 512.9 MB and triggered three DLP alerts, including one for a sensitive keyword match and another for a cloud upload policy violation, both involving Box.com [insider_threat_05_ep_015].

This escalating pattern of excessive data access, repeated large downloads, and multiple DLP violations across different cloud destinations constitutes a clear and growing risk. The behavior is anomalous compared to peers and aligns with data exfiltration tactics, warranting immediate escalation to the security incident response team for further investigation [insider_threat_05_ep_009][insider_threat_05_ep_010][insider_threat_05_ep_012][insider_threat_05_ep_015].
